seed_flags.rationale: Touches the UoA-sensitive topic of Confidentiality; exact alignment is not obvious from the clause text. UoA must be able to disclose to agents and Affiliates. Duration up to 5 years post-completion of confidentiality period. | Acceptable: Confidentiality obligations limited in time (e.g. 5–7 years post-termination/expiry/disclosure). | Escalation: Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts.

seed_flags.clause_title: 5 LIABILITY AND INSURANCE
seed_flags.snippet: 5 LIABILITY AND INSURANCE
seed_flags.rationale: Touches the UoA-sensitive topic of Liability Limitations and Exclusions; exact alignment is not obvious from the clause text. Contract Manager should confirm against: Preferred: Total liability (including under indemnities offered) limited to the lesser of: (a) amount paid under the contract / contract value (subject to liability limit in Research Contract Delegations) OR (b) NZD$500,000. Limited to direct losses caused by negligent acts/omissions or wilful misconduct of the University. Consequential or indirect losses, 'hold harmless', and loss of data/profit/revenue/savings/business/goodwill explicitly excluded. | Acceptable: Total liability limit (including indemnities) in line with Research Contract Delegations. Uncapped liability acceptable ONLY for Trusted Funders on contestable rounds. Indirect losses acceptable only if total exposure is capped per Delegations. Carve-outs from cap acceptable for: Fraud, Wilful default, Personal injury/death, statutory non-excludable liability, and confidentiality (only if client revenue < NZD$150M). | Escalation: Refer Research Grants and Contracts Delegations (and Portfolio Manager for Trusted Funders on non-contestable rounds).

seed_flags.rationale: Topic not addressed in the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions.

seed_flags.rationale: Touches the UoA-sensitive topic of Foreign Currency; exact alignment is not obvious from the clause text. Contract Manager should confirm against: Preferred: Payment made in NZD. | Acceptable: Foreign currencies under NZD$500,000 from countries with FX rates listed by FIRST. | Escalation: UoA Treasury via FIRST (for foreign currencies > NZD$500,000 or unlisted FX rates).

seed_flags.snippet: 8. SUSPENSION OR TERMINATION
seed_flags.rationale: Touches the UoA-sensitive topic of Termination; exact alignment is not obvious from the clause text. Contract Manager should confirm against: Preferred: Mutual two-way termination for material breach of contract or insolvency. Clear right of UoA to terminate for client's failure to pay. Accrued rights and benefits not affected. Client pays termination costs including any uncancellable obligations. | Acceptable: Termination for convenience acceptable ONLY for Consulting contracts, with reasonable notice, PI agreement, and client paying termination costs / uncancellable obligations. Cannot always alter termination provisions for NZ and International Funding Agency contracts (escalate to PM). | Escalation: Senior Manager Research Grants and Contracts.

augmented_flags.clause_title: 1 THE SERVICES
augmented_flags.snippet: 1 THE SERVICES
augmented_flags.rationale: The clause addresses the topic of 'Services,' which is not covered by the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or any registered UoA Standard Template for this contract type. As such, it falls outside the scope of the policy framework.

augmented_flags.clause_title: 1.1 The CONTRACTOR shall perform the Services in accordance with the provisions
augmented_flags.snippet: 1.1 The CONTRACTOR shall perform the Services in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement. If the CONTRACTOR becomes aware of anything that will materially affect the scope or timing of the Services, the CONTRAC…
augmented_flags.rationale: The clause addresses the contractor's obligation to perform services and notify of material changes, which is not covered by any UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or standard template for this contract type. As such, it falls outside the scope of the documented positions.

augmented_flags.clause_title: 1.2 In providing the Services the CONTRACTOR shall exercise the degree of skill,
augmented_flags.snippet: 1.2 In providing the Services the CONTRACTOR shall exercise the degree of skill, care, diligence and foresight reasonably expected from a skilled and experienced provider. The CONTRACTOR is hereby given notice that [redacted] is relying on the accuracy, competence and completeness of the Services and upon the lawful performance of the services by the CONTRACTOR. The CONTRACTOR shall keep records of information and material gathered or created in performing the Services. If any Services provided by the CONTRACTOR are defective or wholly or partly underperformed then [redacted] may require the CONTRACTOR to rectify, perform or re-perform these Services at the CONTRACTOR's expense.
augmented_flags.rationale: The clause addresses the standard of care, reliance on the contractor's performance, record-keeping, and rectification of defective services. These topics are not covered by the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or any registered template for this contract type. As such, the clause falls outside the scope of the UoA Positions document and is flagged as blue.

augmented_flags.clause_title: 1.3 Any Key Personnel specified in the Specific Terms shall give first priority
augmented_flags.snippet: 1.3 Any Key Personnel specified in the Specific Terms shall give first priority to the Services, and shall not become involved in any other project or matter that may adversely affect their ability to provide the Services. The written approval of [redacted] shall be obtained by the CONTRACTOR before any such Key Personnel can be replaced or substituted.
augmented_flags.rationale: The clause addresses the prioritization of Key Personnel and their replacement, which is not covered by any UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or the UoA Standard Template. As such, it falls outside the scope of the documented policies and is flagged as blue.

augmented_flags.snippet: 2.2 The CONTRACTOR shall issue an invoice to [redacted] for payments due and shall include a breakdown of costs and Disbursements (copies of receipts for Disbursements will be made available to [redacted] on request). This deviation triggers escalation to the Research Contract Delegations for review.

augmented_flags.clause_title: 8. SUSPENSION OR TERMINATION
augmented_flags.snippet: 8. SUSPENSION OR TERMINATION
augmented_flags.rationale: The clause addresses the topic of Termination, which is a UoA-sensitive area. However, the clause text provided is too vague to confirm alignment with UoA's Preferred or Acceptable positions. UoA Position #POS-19 specifies that mutual two-way termination for material breach or insolvency is preferred, with clear rights for UoA to terminate for non-payment and provisions for termination costs. Termination for convenience is only acceptable for Consulting contracts under specific conditions. The clause should be reviewed in full context to ensure compliance with these requirements.

augmented_flags.clause_title: 8.1 [REDACTED] may at any time on written notice to the CONTRACTOR suspend the
augmented_flags.snippet: 8.1 [REDACTED] may at any time on written notice to the CONTRACTOR suspend the provision of all or part of the Services or terminate this Agreement by written notice to the CONTRACTOR. The CONTRACTOR shall use its best endeavours to immediately cease providing the Services, and shall arrange for the orderly cessation of Services and shall not incur any further expenditure on the Services. Unless such suspension or termination has been ordered by [REDACTED] with cause, or as a result of breach by the CONTRACTOR, [REDACTED] shall pay the CONTRACTOR the amount of fees and any Disbursements incurred to the date of such suspension or termination.
augmented_flags.rationale: The clause allows for unilateral termination or suspension by the client without specifying that it is limited to material breach, insolvency, or failure to pay by the contractor. This deviates materially from the UoA Preferred Position, which requires mutual two-way termination for material breach or insolvency, and clear rights for UoA to terminate for non-payment. Termination for convenience is only acceptable for Consulting contracts under specific conditions, which are not met here. The clause also lacks explicit provisions for the client to pay termination costs, including uncancellable obligations, which is a key requirement. This clause falls outside the Acceptable tier and requires escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts.

augmented_flags.snippet: 8.2 Upon completion of the Services or sooner termination or suspension of this Agreement the CONTRACTOR shall deliver to [REDACTED] all documents, records, materials (whether completed or not, or in physical or electronic form) gathered, prepared or provided in performing the Services and shall return to [REDACTED] all materials, data and information provided by [REDACTED] to the CONTRACTOR to enable performance of the Services.
augmented_flags.rationale: This clause primarily addresses the return of materials and documents upon completion, termination, or suspension of the agreement. While it mentions termination, it does not directly address the termination rights, obligations, or conditions outlined in UoA Position #POS-19. Instead, it focuses on operational obligations post-termination or completion. This topic is not explicitly covered by any UoA Position or template, and therefore falls outside the scope of the existing policy framework.

While it does not explicitly address termination costs or uncancellable obligations, the clause does not contradict these requirements either. The clause is therefore consistent with the Preferred tier for Termination.

augmented_flags.clause_title: 9. DISPUTE RESOLUTION
augmented_flags.snippet: 9. DISPUTE RESOLUTION
augmented_flags.rationale: The clause text does not provide sufficient detail to confirm alignment with UoA Position #POS-18. The preferred position requires explicit steps: good faith discussions, escalation to senior management, mediation before arbitration/litigation, proceedings in English, and allowance for urgent interlocutory court action. Without these specifics, the clause materially deviates from the preferred and acceptable tiers. Escalation to Senior Manager Research Grants and Contracts or General Counsel is required to ensure compliance.

augmented_flags.clause_title: If a dispute arises out of or related to this Agreement [REDACTED] and the
augmented_flags.snippet: If a dispute arises out of or related to this Agreement [REDACTED] and the CONTRACTOR shall first endeavour, in good faith and without delay, to resolve the dispute by mediation. In the event the dispute is not settled by mediation within 20 days, or such other period as agreed to in writing by [REDACTED] and the CONTRACTOR, the dispute will be referred to arbitration under the Arbitration Act 1996. There will be a single arbitrator and the arbitration will be held in Wellington, New Zealand, and the CONTRACTOR will abide by any decision of the arbitrator. The cost of arbitration proceedings shall be borne as directed by the arbitrator.
augmented_flags.rationale: The clause aligns partially with UoA Position #POS-18 on Dispute Resolution. It includes good faith mediation and arbitration, which are consistent with the preferred tier. However, it does not explicitly mention escalation to senior management before mediation or arbitration, nor does it address the requirement that the process must not prevent urgent interlocutory action through courts. Additionally, the clause specifies arbitration in Wellington, New Zealand, which is acceptable under the governing law and jurisdiction preferences. The clause does not materially deviate from the acceptable tier but lacks full alignment with the preferred tier. Escalation to the Senior Manager Research Grants and Contracts or General Counsel is recommended for confirmation.

augmented_flags.clause_title: 16.3 Give information to [REDACTED] relating to the Services that [REDACTED]
augmented_flags.snippet: 16.3 Give information to [REDACTED] relating to the Services that [REDACTED] reasonably requests, in a format that is usable by [REDACTED] and within a reasonable time of the request.
augmented_flags.rationale: The clause requires the provision of information in a usable format within a reasonable timeframe, but this topic is not addressed in the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or any associated template. As such, it falls outside the scope of the documented positions and is flagged as blue.

augmented_flags.clause_title: 16.4 Co-operate with [REDACTED] to provide information promptly if the
augmented_flags.snippet: 16.4 Co-operate with [REDACTED] to provide information promptly if the information is required by [REDACTED] to comply with an enquiry or its statutory, parliamentary, or other reporting obligations.
augmented_flags.rationale: The clause addresses a topic (cooperation in providing information for statutory or parliamentary reporting obligations) that is not covered by the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or any registered UoA Standard Template for this contract type. As such, it falls outside the scope of the defined policy tiers and is flagged as blue.

augmented_flags.clause_title: 16.5 Make its records available to [REDACTED] during the term of this Agreement
augmented_flags.snippet: 16.5 Make its records available to [REDACTED] during the term of this Agreement and for 7 years after the expiry or termination of this Agreement (unless already provided to [REDACTED] earlier).
augmented_flags.rationale: The clause pertains to record-keeping and post-termination obligations, which are not explicitly covered by any UoA Preferred Contracting Position or template. While it indirectly relates to termination, it does not address termination rights or costs, which are the focus of UoA Position #POS-19. As such, this clause falls outside the scope of the Positions document and requires independent review.
augmented_flags.standard_ref: Not covered by UoA Position or Template

augmented_flags.clause_title: 16.6 Make sure that records provided by, or created for, [REDACTED] are securely
augmented_flags.snippet: 16.6 Make sure that records provided by, or created for, [REDACTED] are securely managed and destroyed on their instructions.
augmented_flags.rationale: The clause addresses the secure management and destruction of records, which is not covered by any UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or associated templates. As such, it falls outside the scope of the documented policies and is flagged as blue.

augmented_flags.clause_title: 17 COUNTERPARTS
augmented_flags.snippet: 17 COUNTERPARTS
augmented_flags.rationale: The topic of counterparts (execution of the agreement in multiple copies or by electronic means) is not addressed in the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions, and there is no UoA Standard Template for this contract type. As such, this clause falls outside the scope of the documented positions.

augmented_flags.clause_title: This Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts, including scanned
augmented_flags.snippet: This Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts, including scanned and emailed copies. So long as each party has received a counterpart or counterparts carrying the signatures of each of the other parties, …
augmented_flags.rationale: The topic of execution in counterparts, including scanned and emailed copies, is not addressed in the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions. As there is no specific guidance or template for this topic, it falls outside the scope of the policy framework.
